conceptualization.theme_description: Explore the lush, dense layers of tropical foliage.

techniques.description: Utilize the color bleeding technique to mimic the merging hues of the dense and lush tropical forest. This technique allows pigments to flow into one another creating seamless transitions, capturing the essence of a humid, sunlit canopy.

Apply the wet-on-wet technique to create soft undefined edges and shapes, echoing the mystery and depth of the foliage. This method will be particularly useful to suggest leaves and trees shrouded in mist.

Implement multiple layers of translucent paints to build depth and intensity, reflecting the complexity and vibrancy of the tropical vegetation. Each layer will represent various elements of the forest, from foreground foliage to distant greenery.
Use splattering to create specks of light and texture, resembling sunlight piercing through the dense tree tops.
Incorporate dripping to suggest rain or water sources in the environment, enhancing the damp and lush atmosphere of the tropical landscape.
